image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Genes are read into messenger RNA before proteins are built.
Context visual: This process, called transcription, is the first step in protein synthesis.
Pattern Interrupt visual: But how does the DNA know what to say? It’s all in the sequence!
Escalation visual: RNA polymerase reads the DNA strand and builds a complementary RNA strand.
Evidence visual: This RNA strand carries the genetic instructions from the nucleus to the ribosomes.
Payoff visual: And that's how your body reads its blueprints to create proteins!
Cta visual: Want to dive deeper into genetics? Hit that follow button!
